input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-28T00:59:21.570Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or a factory method:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
//                 property                  light value  dark value

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
                 Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
                 Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` is the programmatic equivalent of `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}`.

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light`/`Dark`/`Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Both approaches update instantly when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Resources** | For reuse, define themed values in `AppTheme` resource dictionaries in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` |
| **Override** | Force a theme with `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` |
